name: Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop
name_original: Luanco, Cabo Peñas y Mina Llumeres — circular por la ruta de la costa
description: Cycle the moderate 20.9-mile Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop for coastal views, the Cabo Peñas Lighthouse, and varied terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Gozón, a municipality in Asturias, Spain, features a diverse landscape characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs, varied terrain, and charming fishing villages. The region's routes often follow the Cantabrian Sea, offering expansive views and a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. Gozón's geography includes iconic landmarks such as Cabo Peñas, the northernmost point of Asturias, which features a lighthouse atop rugged cliffs. The area provides a range of cycling experiences, from coastal paths to more challenging inland routes. Best touring cycling routes around Gozón The most popular touring cycling route is Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal vistas and varied terrain around Cabo Peñas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cabo Peñas Lighthouse – Cabo Peñas  loop from Verdicio, a difficult 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route features rugged cliffs and views of the lighthouse overlooking the Cantabrian Sea. Local touring cyclists also love the Luanco – Cabo Peñas  loop from Ayuntamiento de Gozón, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and past charming villages, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Gozón is defined by coastal paths, rugged cliffs, and picturesque villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to more challenging loops with significant elevation gain. The routes in Gozón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gozón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Gozón?
A: Gozón offers a diverse selection of 25 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy coastal paths to more challenging loops.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Gozón?
A: The routes in Gozón are categorized by difficulty to suit different riders. You'll find 4 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 6 more difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for every experience level.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see while cycling in Gozón?
A: Gozón is renowned for its dramatic coastal landscapes. Many routes offer stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea, particularly around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4016988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, the northernmost point of Asturias. You can also enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/391360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa Torres Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and explore diverse beaches such as Verdicio and Xagó.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Gozón?
A: Yes, many of Gozón's touring cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029990998/luanco-cabo-penas-cyclotourist-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse – Cabo Peñas 🌊 loop from Verdicio&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Gozón's touring cycling routes?
A: Gozón's terrain is varied, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter coastal paths with sea breezes, rugged cliffs, and routes that weave through charming villages. This diversity provides a rich cycling experience, from smooth stretches to more challenging sections with elevation changes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Gozón?
A: Yes, Gozón offers several routes suitable for families, particularly the easier coastal paths that provide gentle gradients and beautiful scenery. These routes allow for a relaxed pace, perfect for enjoying the views together.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Gozón?
A: The touring cycling routes in Gozón are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore charming villages along the way.

Q: Can I explore charming towns or villages along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Gozón is home to picturesque fishing villages like Luanco, the capital, known for its stone streets and maritime heritage. Many routes pass through or near these towns, offering opportunities to visit historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St Thomas of Canterbury&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy local gastronomy.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Gozón?
A: While specific public transport connections to every trailhead may vary, towns like Luanco and Candás, which serve as starting points for several routes, are generally accessible by local bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a good mix of coastal scenery and village exploration?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candás – Luanco loop from Ayuntamiento de Gozón&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent moderate option that combines beautiful coastal views with the chance to explore two charming Asturian fishing villages. This route allows you to experience both the natural beauty and cultural richness of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Gozón?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Gozón, with milder temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the coast, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather and more visitors.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions to visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, Gozón offers several historical and cultural points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St Thomas of Canterbury&lt;/a&gt; in Luanco, or explore ancient burial mounds like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Areo VI Burial Mounds&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from some routes.
tips: A fishing village with a secluded port and beautiful beaches. It is worth strolling through the old town, with its Clock Tower. Luanco also has a wonderful promenade where at the end of La Marina beach you will find the church of Santa María.

The Cabo Peñas Lighthouse is located on the most salient plateau of Cabo de Peñas, 100 meters from sea level and about 60 meters from the cliff.
In operation since 1852, it was electrified in 1946. In 1951 a siren was installed that emits the letter "P" in Morse, replaced in 1977. In 1956 a radio beacon was installed, a radio station that broadcasts in Morse PS, and in 2003 a Dgps station. Emits 3 white flashes every 15 sec. Reaching 21 miles.

An iron mine that already worked in Roman times and until 1967. Now practically only ruins remain. A small recreational area and a viewpoint have been built at the entrance to the descent to the beach.
Nice beach with golden sand and calm waters. Attached to the beach there is a restaurant with views of the beach.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop?
faqs.answer: The full loop, as described, typically takes around 2 hours and 22 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. However, if you consider the core Luanco to Cabo Peñas section as an out-and-back, it can take approximately 3.5 hours for the round trip, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Luanco - Cabo Peñas route is generally considered easy or to have low difficulty. It's accessible to a wide range of touring cyclists, including families. While there are some moderate inclines and varied terrain, it's not overly challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cyclotourist loop?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, combining paved sections (asphalt) with stretches of gravel and sand. You'll encounter descents and ascents, but the overall elevation changes are moderate. Bikes with wider tires, like mountain bikes, might be more comfortable on the gravel and sand portions.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning coastal scenery with dramatic cliffs and views of the Cantabrian Sea. Key points of interest include the charming town of Luanco, the iconic Cabo Peñas Lighthouse, the recreational area of the Llumeres Iron Mine ⚒️, and beautiful beaches like Playa de Bañugues. On clear days, you might even spot the distant Picos de Europa mountains.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends in Luanco. You can find parking within the town to begin your ride. As it's a loop, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable for much of the year, but the best time is generally during the spring or autumn when the weather is mild and the crowds are smaller. Summer offers warmer temperatures but can be busier. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Luanco - Cabo Peñas Cyclotourist Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas and beaches, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock farms. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. The trail is located within the Paisaje Protegido del Cabo Peñas, a protected landscape, but it is freely accessible to the public for recreational activities like cycling.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll cycle along parts of the Senda Costera Faro San Juan de Nieva - Cabo Peñas and the Senda Costera Cabo Peñas - Luanco, among others.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several small villages and towns, including Luanco, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These provide good opportunities for breaks, refreshments, or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its ornithological interest, making it appealing for birdwatchers. You'll also pass by livestock farms, and the coastal vegetation is typical of the Cantabrian coastline, with various wildflowers depending on the season.
